name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax Title Loans offers car title loans up to $10,000 using your vehicle as collateral. Founded in 1990, with multiple Michigan locations and fast in-store cash access.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, GA and has operated for over 35 years as a national title lending company. Their complaint and administrative address is listed in Alpharetta, GA (Select Management Group). They are licensed and regulated in multiple states including Michigan, Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Nevada, with state-specific disclosures and license links provided on their website. The Michigan Department of Insurance and Financial Services oversees their Michigan operations.

LoanMax specializes exclusively in auto title loans — short-term loans secured by the equity in a borrower's vehicle. To qualify at any location, borrowers need only their vehicle, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear vehicle title (free of liens). Loans are advertised up to $10,000, though approval and amount are subject to vehicle evaluation and ability to repay. The Southgate, MI location at 12850 Eureka Road serves the broader metro Detroit area and accepts Cash, Check, Debit Card, and Western Union as payment methods. Additional nearby Michigan locations include Taylor, Garden City, and two Detroit locations.

What distinguishes LoanMax from purely online title lenders is its brick-and-mortar network with consistent operating hours: Monday through Friday 10:00AM–6:00PM and Saturday 9:00AM–2:00PM. Customers can walk in, apply, and receive funds the same day without a credit check requirement (the vehicle is the collateral). The company offers an online registration and payment portal in addition to in-store service, and publishes state-specific regulatory disclosures and rate schedules for Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Nevada directly on its website.

Honest assessment: LoanMax is a high-cost lender by nature of the product category. The Southgate location page does not disclose interest rates or APR, which makes cost comparison difficult before visiting. Their own Nevada disclosure warns that 'title loans should be used for short-term financial needs only and not as a long-term financial solution,' and advises customers with credit difficulties to seek credit counseling first. The vehicle is at risk of repossession if the borrower defaults. LoanMax is a legitimate, licensed operation with a long track record, but the product is expensive and carries collateral risk — it is appropriate only as a last resort for borrowers who have exhausted lower-cost options.
address: 1694 White Bear Ave Ste 103, Maplewood, MN 55109

company_info.headquarters: 1694 White Bear Ave Ste 103, Maplewood, MN 55109

pros: 35+ years in operation — founded 1990, one of the longest-tenured title lenders in the US
Loans up to $10,000, higher ceiling than most payday or emergency cash lenders
Minimal requirements: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title — no credit check required
Four payment methods accepted: Cash, Check, Debit Card, and Western Union
Saturday hours (9AM–2PM) accommodate borrowers who cannot visit on weekdays
Licensed and regulated in multiple states with publicly posted rate schedules and license links
Multiple metro Detroit locations (Southgate, Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, Center Line) for local access
cons: Interest rates and APR are not disclosed on the location or homepage — borrowers must visit in-store or call to get cost details
Your vehicle is collateral — failure to repay can result in repossession
Company's own Nevada disclosure acknowledges this is not a long-term financial solution
Limited hours (closes 6PM weekdays, 2PM Saturday) compared to 24/7 online lenders
No indication of a grace period, rollover policy, or early payoff terms on the public website
best_for: Vehicle owners who need fast access to cash ($500–$10,000) and hold a clear, lien-free title
Borrowers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for a personal loan or credit card
Michigan residents who prefer in-person service over online-only lenders
People facing a one-time short-term cash shortfall who can repay quickly to avoid prolonged interest
diagnosis: LoanMax is best for vehicle owners in Michigan who need $500–$10,000 quickly and cannot access cheaper credit options such as personal loans or credit union PALs. The main caveat is that the vehicle serves as collateral and can be repossessed on default, and the company does not publicly disclose APR or fee schedules for Michigan on their location pages — making true cost comparison impossible without contacting the store directly.
services: Car title loans (auto title loans) up to $10,000
Online loan application and registration portal
Multi-location network across metro Detroit, MI
State-licensed operations with posted regulatory disclosures
Vehicle equity evaluation for loan approval
